Math_Problem:
Determine all positive integers $N$ for which the sphere
\[x^2 + y^2 + z^2 = N\]
has an inscribed regular tetrahedron whose vertices have integer coordinates. Instead of determining all positive integers $N$ for which the sphere admits an inscribed regular tetrahedron with integer-coordinate vertices, simply find $b$, where $b$ is the denominator of the side length of the cube formed by the tetrahedron's vertices and their point reflections through the sphere's center, when that side length is expressed as a reduced fraction derived from the integrality condition on the cube's volume. Consider those functions $ f: \mathbb{N} \mapsto \mathbb{N}$ which satisfy the condition
\[ f(m + n) \geq f(m) + f(f(n)) - b \]
for all $ m,n \in \mathbb{N}.$ Find all possible values of $ f(b + 2006)$.

Connecting_Sentence.A3226: Instead of determining all positive integers $N$ for which the sphere admits an inscribed regular tetrahedron with integer-coordinate vertices, simply find $b$, where $b$ is the denominator of the side length of the cube formed by the tetrahedron's vertices and their point reflections through the sphere's center, when that side length is expressed as a reduced fraction derived from the integrality condition on the cube's volume.

Determine all positive integers $N$ for which the sphere
\[x^2 + y^2 + z^2 = N\]
has an inscribed regular tetrahedron whose vertices have integer coordinates. Instead of determining all positive integers $N$ for which the sphere admits an inscribed regular tetrahedron with integer-coordinate vertices, simply find $e = b + 2006$, where $b$ is the denominator of the side length of the cube formed by the tetrahedron's vertices and their point reflections through the sphere's center, when that side length is expressed as a reduced fraction derived from the integrality condition on the cube's volume.

Synthesised_By.A3957:
Consider those functions $ f: \mathbb{N} \mapsto \mathbb{N}$ which satisfy the condition
\[ f(m \plus{} n) \geq f(m) \plus{} f(f(n)) \minus{} 1
\]
for all $ m,n \in \mathbb{N}.$ Find all possible values of $ f(2007).$

[i]Author: Nikolai Nikolov, Bulgaria[/i]

Original_Problem.A3226:
Determine all positive integers $N$ for which the sphere
\[x^2 + y^2 + z^2 = N\]
has an inscribed regular tetrahedron whose vertices have integer coordinates.

Original_Problem.A3957:
Consider those functions $ f: \mathbb{N} \mapsto \mathbb{N}$ which satisfy the condition
\[ f(m \plus{} n) \geq f(m) \plus{} f(f(n)) \minus{} 1
\]
for all $ m,n \in \mathbb{N}.$ Find all possible values of $ f(2007).$

[i]Author: Nikolai Nikolov, Bulgaria[/i]

A3226: Mathematics -> Number Theory -> Perfect Numbers (as the problem contextually involves understanding integer conditions and perfect squares indirectly) -> Other

Claims.expr: All positive integers N s.t. exists regular tetrahedron with integer vertices inscribed in x^2 + y^2 + z^2 = N
Inscribed means vertices lie on the sphere
If N = 3m^2 for m positive integer, then points (m,m,m), (m,-m,-m), (-m,m,-m), (-m,-m,m) are integer points on the sphere
These 4 points form a regular tetrahedron
Thus N = 3m^2 for positive integer m satisfy the property
Assume such P1 = (x1,y1,z1), P2, P3, P4 exist with integer coordinates forming regular tetrahedron
Q_i = (-x_i, -y_i, -z_i) for i=1 to 4 are integer points on the sphere
Points P1..P4, Q1..Q4 form vertices of inscribed cube
U1 = Q2 - Q1, U2 = Q3 - Q1, U3 = Q4 - Q1 are integer vectors
Let M be 3x3 matrix with rows U1, U2, U3; det(M) integer
Let t = (N/3)^{1/2}; then t^3 integer and t^2 = N/3 rational
t = a/b with a, b positive integers, gcd(a,b)=1
b^3 divides a^3; since gcd(a,b)=1, b=1
Positive integers N are exactly those of form 3m^2 for positive integer m
orall m,n \in \mathbb{N}, f(m + n) \geq f(m) + f(n) - 1
